def: s.f. A very fine species of cotton cloth (made originally at Ćandel or Canderī. The thread of the best quality is sold for its weight in silver; and the cloth is of so costly a description as to be used in native courts only).
more: n. f. 1. A very fine cotton cloth. ☞ It was originally fabricated at Chanderī , on the left bank of the Betwa, from a kind of cotton grown at Amrāoti in Behar. The thread, when of fine quality, is sold for its weight in silver, and the cloth is of so costly a description as to be worn only by persons of the highest rank in native courts. Wilson . The weavers work in a dark subterranean room, of which the walls are kept damp to prevent dust from flying about. Carnegy .

more: n. f. 1. Silver. 2. Silver or electro-plate; the household plate. 3. Wealth; money; riches. Kamātī hai bahan, khātā hai woh, chāṅdī hai bhaṛve kī Wom. Rāhat. The sister earns, the pimp of a brother lives in clover. 4. Profits; gains; emoluments; perquisites; bribes. 1. Unkī to āj kal chāṅdī hai . 2. Nahar par chāṅdī kā meṅh baras rahā hai . Showers of silver (bribes) rain on the Canals. chāṅdī kā pahrā , n. m. A lucky moment; ( Met .) Floodtide. chāṅdī kā jūtā , n. m. lit . a silver shoe. A bribe; hush money. chāṅdī kā waraq , n. m. Thin silver leaf. chāṅdī kaṭnā, yā galnā , v. n. 1. To be spent, consumed (riches). Mukān banāne meṅ ek zāt kī chāṅdī kaṭtī hai . A heap of money goes in house-building. 2. To acquire money; make a fortune. chāṅdī kar denā , v. n. To burn to ashes. Ek hī dam meṅ sulfā chāṅdī kar dīyā. chāṅdī ke patre , n. m. 1. Thin plates of silver. 2. Any thing very white. …
s. f. 1. Silver (pure), plate. 2. The crown of the head.
s. (dakh.) Hair of the top of the head gathered up and tied ( Harris ).

def: s.f. Moonlight, moonbeam; — a kind of white flower, Tabernæmontana coronaria; — a white cloth spread over a carpet; an awning, a canopy or cope; anything white and shining
more: n. f. S. चन्द्रिका Pr. chandimā ; Pālī, chandikā . 1. Moonlight; moonbeams. 1. Woh chhiṭkī huī chāṅdnī jā bajā, Woh jāṛe kī āmad, woh ṭhanḍī havā . Mīr Hasan. 2. Chāṅdnī meṅ shahad nahīṅ hotā . Bees dont make honey in moonlight. (It would be stolen from them). 3. Chāṅdnī meṅ fasd khulvānī manā hai . Prov. The barber surgeon does not bleed during the bright fortnight of the moon. 2. The white flower; tabernœmontana coronaria , Wat. 3. A white cloth spread over a carpet. Tale se kheṅch le masnad ko ān-kar farrāsh, Agar kaheṅ ki miṭā uṭh-ke chāṅdnī kā jhol . Saudā. The cloth from under you the attendant will draw quite, If you but ask him just to smooth its creases right. chāṅdnī rāt , n. f. A moonlight night. chāṅdnī mār jānā , v. n. To be paralyzed from a stroke of the moon ( Com . a horse). Ghoṛe ko chāṅdnī mār gaī . chāṅdnī-karan , n. m. The practice of Brāhmans, etc. wounding themselve…

Ghabrā-ke mere marz se yūṅ bole tabīb, 'āh! Chaṅgā nahīṅ hotā hai ye bīmār, kareṅ kyā?' Jurat. 3. Pure; truthful; true; right. 1. Chaṅgī gal . 2. Man chaṅgā to kaṭhautī meṅ Gaṅgā . Prov. def: s.m. One who plays on the ćaṅg or harp, &c., a harper.
more: n. f. H. chuṭkī a pinch. Weighman's fees: a cess levied from grain-sellers, etc.; town duties ( paun-toṭī ). ☞ A handful of grain levied as a tax or fee for weighing, or as a compensation for the use of market conveniences, as bags, booths, etc. Similar contributions to religious mendicants or allowance to zamindars for establishing a new market or permitting a fair to be held. chungī-peṅṭh , n. A market or fair held on the tenure of giving a small portion of each saleable article to the zamindār .
s. f. A tax gathered daily from grain merchants, being as much grain as a man can grasp in his hand.
s. f. 1. See چنگاري chingārī. 2. A wicked dwarf. چنگي جهڙنا چهوٿنا يا نکلنا chingī-jhaṛnā, -chhūṭnā or -nikalnā, To sparkle.
s. m. One who plays on the چنگ chang, q. v. A harper, &c.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Healthy, sound, robust; expert, active, smart

def: s.m. Claw (of a wild beast); talon (of a bird); hand; clutch, grasp; hook; — a handful or fist-full (of anything dry).

s. m. Claw, talon, hand, clutch, grasp, hook.

more: n. f. S. शोण A small ruby. Gehūṅ kyā, chunniyāṅ haiṅ! Not wheat, call it a ruby bright!
s. f. 1. A small ruby. 2. Ascarides.

more: n. m. 1. A stamp or mark. See thāp . 2. The ceremony so-called. See Carnegy , p. 75.

more: n. f. 1. The bursting of the husk of pods by the heat of the sun. 2. See chik. Kamar meṅ chanak ā gaī .

ćakkī jhonā, To turn or work a hand-mill, to grind

s.f. Presentation of perfumed powder to the bride and bridegroom and the women by whom it has been prepared.

ety: see ćāṅk; and cf. ćapkānā, and ćāṅpnā
def: v.t. To stamp or mark.

more: n. f. S. चाप 1. A bow. 2. The lock of a gun. 3. Michelia kisopa , Wat. chāṅp chaṛhānā , v. n. 1. To cock a gun; to bend the bow. Shām gāt, sir jaṭā baṛhāë, Arun nain, sar chāṅp chaṛhāë . Rāmāyan. 2. To punish or torture by squeezing the ear under the lock of a gun; to torture.
s. f. 1. The lock of a gun. 2. The stocks, the rack. چانپ چڙهانا chāmp chaṛhānā, 1. To cock a gun. 2. To punish or torment by squeezing the ear with the lock of a gun.
subs: (-kī), To bend a bow; to cock a gun; — to punish or torture by squeezing the ear under the lock of a gun; to put in the stocks.

def: s.f. The tree Michelia kisopa (cf. ćampā).
s.m. Kneaded flour, dough.
more: n. The kneaded flour before it is put in the oven and baked; dough.

def: s.f. (dialec.), A key ( = ćābī, q.v.). s.f. (local) A key (syn. tāl).

more: v. a. 1. To join. 2. To stuff, to cram, to thrust in, to press.

def: s.m. Uncooked rice ( = ćāwal, q.v.) s.m. Rice cleared of the husk and not cooked (when boiled it is termed bhāt); — the weight of a grain of rice, one-eighth of a rattī, q.v.
more: n. m. S. चण्डुल 1. Rice separated from the husk. 2. One eighth of a rattī , q. v. chāṅval chabvānā , v. a. To cause persons suspected of theft to chew uncooked rice, a deficiency of saliva being held to indicate the thief.
subs: (-se), To make (one) chew rice (over which an incantation has been pronounced: this is done in the case of persons suspected of stealing, it being believed that the thief will be discovered by blood issuing from his mouth, or by a deficiency of saliva being produced.

def: s.m. A small species of millet.
s.m. The Bos grunniens, &c. ( = ćamar, q.v.); — the male flower of maize
more: n. m. S. चमर a chaurī , Pr. chamaram, chāmaram . 1. The tail of the Bos grunniens; the yak; a flapper to shake off flies, etc; a sign of royalty. Bāloṅ bāṅdhī ek chhināl, Nit voh rahtī khole bāl, Dhanī ko chhoṛ chākar se rājī, Chatrā ho so arth bichārī . Riddle. Ans. Chauṅrī . A fly flapper. A female frail, with hairs she's strung, All night and day her hair loose hung, Her spouse she leaves, to servants clung— Who wit possess can give her tongue. 2. The male flower of maize (E. dhan bāl ). 3. E. A field. chaṅvar ḍholnā , v. a. To drive off flies with a chaurī .
s. m. The tail of the Bos grunniens, a species of ox, used to whisk off flies, &c. See چونري chauṅrī.
subs: To shake a ćaṅwar or whisk (for the purpose of driving away flies, &c.), to whisk off flies with a ćaurī.

def: adj. & s.m. Left (hand or side); discordant, inharmonious; unlucky, sinister; — the left hand, the left side

n. f. S. चुप Silence ( khāmoshī ); stillness. 1. Ek chup, sau ko harāë . Prov. One man who keeps silent will defeat a hundred who talk. 2. Sab se bhalī chup . Prov. Silence is best. 3. Chup kī dād Khudā degā . Prov. God will avenge the wrongs in silence borne. 4. Bārū jaise kirkirī, ujjal jaise dhup, Aisī mīṭhī kuchh nahīṅ jaisī mīṭhī chup . Dohā. As crisp as sand and as the sun full bright, But nothing is so sweet as silence quite. 5. Chup ādhī marzī . Prov. Silence is half-consent. chup, chup-rah, choṅch band kar ; P. khāmosh , intj. Hold your tongue! silence! peace! chup chāp, chup chupāte , adv. Silently; quite still; stealthily; secretly; peaceably. chup chup , adj. Silent; mute; dumb. Phr. Still as a mouse; still as a statue. chup k. , v. a. 1. To preserve silence; to stop any noise. 2. To confute; silence; shut up. chupke bāham salāh k . To consult or conspire together in secret; …
adj. 1. Silent. 2. interj. Silence! (the word ره being understood). 3. s. f. Silence: as, چپ لگني يا چپکي لگني chup lagnī or chupkī lagnī, v. n. To be struck dumb.
adv. (dakh.) Gratis, for nothing, uselessly ( Harris ).
subs: To run zigzag when pursued by an enemy (in order to baffle him), to dodge
adj., adv. & s.m. Right and left; — carelessness; unsteadiness, want of principle.

def: s.m. The refuse of the jhaṛ-berī after the pālā is beaten from it
subs: s.m. Gross measurement of the lands of an estate.
wety: From Sanskrit चाप (cāpa).

def: s.f. = ćabak, ṭapak, qq.v. s.f. Throbbing, throb, shoot of pain ( = ṭabhak; ṭapak, qq.v.). s.f. Sound of (water, &c.) dropping; dropping; beat, throb, pulsation, &c. ( = ṭapak, q.v.).
more: n. So called from its note: The goatsucker; caprimulgus . They breed on the ground, making no nest and often lay in a rut. chip'pa/?/kh , adj. Thin; sunken. Biyāh hote hī gāl chippakh ho gaë . No sooner wed than his cheeks all sunken grew.
adj. 1. Shallow. 2. s. f. Name of a bird.

ety: ćapak˚ = Prk. चपक्क(इ) or चपक्के(इ), fr. S. चप or चर्प+कृ
def: v.n. To be compressed; to be pressed flat down or on; to collapse; to stick, adhere; to cling (to); — to take up (with), form an unlawful connexion (with), to be entangled (in an amour), to fall in love (with); to spread or sink (as ink on damp paper)
more: v. a. 1. To paste; gum; to stick on. 2. To put up or affix (a notice). 3. To provide for; get one a berth.
v. n. 1. To stick; adhere. 2. See ulajhnā , 9, 10. chipakvānā , Caus. of chipkānā , q. v.

v. n. 1. To stick, to adhere. 2. To spread or sink (as, ink on damp paper). 3. To be compressed. چپک جانا chipak jānā, v. a. 1. To be sold on account of its sweetness. 2. To fall in love at first sight.
subs: v.n. (intens.) To stick close (to), to stick together; to be compressed, &c. = ćipaknā; — to be sold on account of (its) sweetness (cf. pićaknā, and ćipaṭnā).

def: s.m. Fickleness, unsteadiness; mobility, quick movement, quickness, swiftness.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā) & adv. Shaking, trembling, tremulous, unsteady, agitated, restless, wavering; variable, inconstant, fickle, uncertain, volatile, giddy, wanton; active, quick, swift, agile, expeditious; swiftly, quickly, &c.; — an unsteady person, &c.
more: adj. S. चपल 1. Quick; brisk; glancing; vivid; brilliant. 2. Wanton ( achpal , 3.). Komal gāt, umar hai bālī, chaṅdrmukhī, chaplā, chanchal . Song. Lāonī . As velvet soft, youthful, moon-faced, mincing and skittish too.
adj. 1. Tremulous, shaking. 2. Wanton, restless, volatile.
subs: adj. Of a fickle or inconstant nature
wety: Borrowed from Telugu చెప్పులు (ceppulu), the plural of చెప్పు (ceppu).

def: adj. Foul, filthy, nastly, disgusting, brutal, beastly.
wety: Borrowed from Telugu చెప్పులు (ceppulu), the plural of చెప్పు (ceppu).

def: s.f. Lightning; the goddess Lakshmī or Fortune; a wanton, a harlot.
more: Poet. n. f. 1. Lightning. Chaplā chamkan lāgī, chāro disā, nis meṅ jugnū darsan lāge . Song. On every side the lightnings flashed and glowworms brightly shone. 2. A wanton; a whore. 3. An epithet of the goddess Lakshmī

v.t. To cause (one) to feel shame, to abash; — to place one thing on another, to heap, pile; to press down, compress, flatten, squeeze, crush.
more: v. a. To cause to feel shame. Jab roṭiyoṅ ke ba'ne kā ā-kar paṭe shumār, Muflis ko ek deṅ, to tavaṅggar ko ch/?/ār chār, Gar aur māṅge voh to use jhiṛkeṅ bār bār, Is muflisī kā āh! bayāṅ kyā karūṅ maiṅ, yār? Muflis ko us jagah bhī chapātī hai muflisī . On poverty. Nazīr. When meal-cakes served out are at times in charity, For one the poor man gets the rich gets over three, If more he asks for with harsh speech browbeat is he, Alas! what words can tell the tale of misery, Even here the poor are brought to shame by poverty.
v. a. 1. To abash. 2. To place one thing on another, to pile.

s.f. = ćapātī, q.v.
s.f. A thin cake of unleavened bread.
more: n. f. S. चर्पटी A thin cake of flour and water, without leaven, slightly baked or toasted over an open fire. 1. Paisā paisā bhar kī chapātī pakātī hai . She bakes thin cakes but one pice weight. 2. ' Tīn chapātī, nau barātī, khāo chūram chūr!' 'Aë ghar-basī! tere biyāh hai, yā lūṭam lūṭ!' 'Bandī jab kartī hai, aisā hī kartī hai .' Wom. Prov. Hostess . 'Three wheaten cakes, nine wedding guests—feast, feast my friends, nor spare.' Neighbour . 'Destroyer of thy house! no marriage feast but plunder rare!' Hostess . 'Your servants' feasts are all like this —a sumptuous affair.' chapātī sā peṭ , A soft stomach.
s. f. A thin cake of unleavened bread.
wety: From Sanskrit चर्पटी (carpaṭī).

s. A sort of vest (like the فبا ḳabā, q. v. but breast covered: Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: s.m. A vest like a ćapkan.

def: s.m. A flatterer, wheedler, a sycopnant.
more: s. m. A flatterer, a wheedler.

def: s.f. Flattery, wheedling; fawning, sycophancy.
more: n. f. Fawning; sycophancy; honeyed words. Chāplūsī kā mūṅh kālā . Prov. Saying. Fawning has a black face.

def: v.t. = ćāṅpnā, q.v. v.t. To press, squeeze, to knead (dough, or the limbs), to shampoo; to join; to thrust in, stuff, cram.

ety: ćap˚ = Prk. चप्प(इ) = S. चप्य(ते), pass. of rt. चप्
def: v.n. To be compressed, be flattened; to be crushed or squeezed; — to be abashed or ashamed; to be bashful; to blush; to lower the eyes, to cower, quail; to submit, stoop; to be put to silence.
more: v. n. S. चप to be quiet. 1. To be silent. 2. To be ashamed, abashed, bashful; to blush. 3. To hang down one's eyes; to blink; cower; quail ( jhepnā ). Jo chap chap-kar āṅkh jhapāve, O ke ran māṅ sel chalāve.? Rus. Prov. Who blink and turn their eyes away with spear in battle ne'er make way. 4. To be shut up; hushed.
v. n. 1. To be abashed, to be bashful in company, to blush, submit, stoop. 2. To be crushed or squeezed. ¶ chipnā (for چهپنا chhipnā, q. v.) v. n. To be concealed.

def: s.f. A flat lid or cover (of a cooking pot; syn. sar-posh; cf. ḍhaknī); a saucer; — the cap of the knee, knee-pan; — the crown of the head
more: 1. A cover ( ḍhaknā ); saucer. Likh-kar chapnī sir par dharī Nikal paṛā yā nikal paṛī . Mah. A charm. An earthen plate writ on and laid upon her head, Delivered of a boy or girl the brought-to-bed. ☞ The legend is that a holy man, called Shaikh Farīd-ud-dīn, had the miraculous power of turning stone into sugar. Hence, he was surnamed Shakar-ganj. The above couplet refers to the custom of inscribing his name, with the above couplet, on an earthen plate and placing it as a charm on the head of the lying-in-woman to facilitate delivery. 2. The knee-pan; knee-cap. 3. The crown of the head. chapnī bhar pānī meṅ ḍūb marnā , v. n. 1. To drown oneself in a spoonful of water. 2. To be greatly abashed; to be ready to die with shame.

s. f. A lid, a cover, top (small). چپني بهرپاني مين يا ليکي ڐوب مرنا chapnī bhar panī meṅ (or leke) ḍūb marnā (lit. to drown oneʼs self in a saucer-full of water) To be greatly abashed. چپني چاٿني chapnī chāṭnī (lit. to lick the pot-lid) To be contented with little.
s. f. Cap (of the knee: Harris ).
subs: (or pānī leke) ḍub-marnā, 'To drown oneself in a saucer-full of water'; to be greatly abashed
'To lick the pot-lid'; to be contented with little.

ety: rt. of ćap-nā+S. पत्रिका prob. oṭī is a diminut. suff.
def: s.f. An old worn-out turban or cap; a flattened hat or cap.
more: s. f. An old worn-out turband.

def: s.m. A hand-breadth, a measure of four fingers, a span (of land)
more: n. m. S. चपट A span of land. Chappe jitnī koṭhṛī, aur mīyāṅ muhalle-dār! Prov. A span of land he occupies, and my gentleman is chief householder forsooth! chappā chappā , adv. Every inch of ground; every stone in the place; every creak and cranny; every hole and corner.
subs: adv. Every span of land, every inch of ground.

def: s.f. A sandal; a slipper.
wety: Borrowed from Telugu చెప్పులు (ceppulu), the plural of చెప్పు (ceppu).

def: s.f. (dialec.), The prickly pear, Cactus indicus (syn. ṅāg-phanī).
s.m. A (large) lid or cover of a pot ( = ćapnī).
more: s. m. A lid or cover of a pot (large).

def: s.f. Pressing or kneading the limbs, shampooing.
more: n. f. Gently kneading the limbs; shampooing.
n. f. A patch or join (on paper).
s. f. A patch on paper.

def: s.m. A paddle, an oar; — (local), a sandal, a shoe with a wooden sole (syn. ćappal)
more: n. m. S. क्षेपणि from क्षिप to throw. An oar; a paddle. chappū mār'nā , v. a. To row.
s. m. A paddle, an oar. چپومارنا chappū mārnā, v. a. To row.

def: s.m. = ćāpṛā, q.v.
s.m. A cake of cow-dung ( = ćapṛī).
adv. Tenaciously, dog, gedly, persistently; suddenly, unexpectedly, all at once.
more: n. m. S. कार्पट 1. Shell lac; lac. 2. Two pice (Brokers)

def: s.m. Shell lac, lac; husk, bran; clear land; — two pice (in the language of brokers).
more: s. m. 1. A kind of lac. 2. Clear land.

def: v.t. = ćipṭānā, q.v. (fr. ćipṭā; see ćapṭānā), v.t To cause to adhere, to stick on (anything, as a wafer, &c.); to put on (a patch); to lay down turf, to turf, to sod.
more: v. a. To assert falsely; falsify ( jhuṭlānā ); belie. Mohe chūṅ chaprāë jāë saë . Rus. chaprā , adv. 1. Persistently; doggedly. 2. All of a sudden; unexpectedly.

def: v.t. = ćapṛānā, q.v. v.t. = ćipṭānā, q.v. (fr. ćipṭā; see ćapṭānā), v.t To cause to adhere, to stick on (anything, as a wafer, &c.); to put on (a patch); to lay down turf, to turf, to sod.
v.t. To assert falsely, to falsify, to belie; to brazen.
more: v. a. To falsify, to brazen.

def: s.f. = H چپڙاسي चपड़ासी ćapṛāsī, s.m. See ćaprās, and ćaprāsī.

ety: said to be contrac. fr. P. ćaprāst; but cf. ćapṭā
def: s.f. A breast-plate, a plate worn on a belt (as a mark of office), a badge; a clasp, a buckle.
more: n. f. Cor. of chap o rāst right and left. 1. A plate worn on a belt as a mark of office; the badge of a peon. 2. ( Fac .) The vulva .
s. f. A buckle, a breastplate, clasp, badge.

def: s.m. One who wears a ćaprās or badge, a peon, orderly, beadle, messenger (of a court, &c.)
more: n. m. A peon; orderly; beadle; messenger of a court. chaprāsī-i-adālat . A bailiff; a process server.
s. m. A messenger or other servant wearing a chaprās.

def: s.m. = H چپڙاس चपड़ास ćapṛās, s.f. See ćaprās, and ćaprāsī.
adj. Brazen-faced, shameless, bold, impudent; — s.m. A slipper.
adv. Quickly, instantly, forthwith.
more: adj. Oiled; oily; greasy; rich. Chupṛī, aur do do! Prov. Buttered, and two (cakes)! (Dainties and plenty). (Said usually of offices in which authority is combined with a large salary). chupṛī bāt , n. f. Flattery; soft or honeyed words. chupṛī roṛī , n. f. Cakes rubbed over with clarified butter.

Mār. n. f. Cakes of cow-dung ((اُپلا)). Chhao ganḍā ke chipṛī lihalā se kā hokhī? Chār man ke lakṛī ne jarāib, je koilo hokhī! Bhoj. What is the good of three annas worth of cow-pats? Burn four maunds of wood and you have the charcoal in the bargain.

def: s.f. A puddle; — a small species of pulse (resembling ćanā); — husk, bran (cf. ćapṛā).
s.f. A cake of cow-dung.
more: adj. f. 1. Oiled, greasy. 2. Smooth, plausible. چپڙي بات chupṛī-bāt, Flattery, soft words. چپڙي روٿي chupṛī-roṭī, Cakes rich with clarified butter.

ety: S. पिच्चट or पिच्चिट+कः, rt. पिच्च्; or per met. चिपट or चिपिट+कः; cf.ćapnā
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Pressed flat, flattened, compressed; flat, even, level; shallow and broad (as a vessel); — a person with a flat nose; — a large flat earthen vessel

def: v.t. To press flat, to flatten, to press even.
more: (caus. of چپٿنا chipaṭnā ) v. a. To sod, to turf, to apply patches, to stick.
(trans. of چپٿنا chapaṭnā ) v. a. To flatten.

def: adj. = ćapṭā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Pressed flat, flattened, compressed; flat, even, level; shallow and broad (as a vessel); — a person with a flat nose; — a large flat earthen vessel
s.f. Congressus libidinosus duarum mulierum ( = ćapaṭ-bāzī)
more: s. f. Congressus mulierum. چپٿي لڙنا يا کهيلنا chapṭī-laṛnā or -khelnā, v. n. Libidini sapphicæ indulgere.

more: s. f. The name of an insect.

ety: Prk. चत्तारि = S.चत्वारि, neut. pl. of चतुर्; — the Pers. ćār is a contrac. of ćahār, q.v.
def: adj. Four
more: adj. S. चत्वार, Z. chathware , P. chahār . Four. 1. Laṛākā ke chār kān . Prov. The pugnacious man has four ears. (He hears twice as much as a peaceable man (to make a quarrel of). 2. Jiske chār bhaiyā, māreṅ dhaul, chhin leṅ rupaiyā . Prov. He who has four brothers may knock you down and take your money. 3. Chār hāth pāoṅ sab ke haiṅ . Prov. Saying. Every one has two arms and two legs. (Two can play at the same game). chār abrū kā safāyā , Mah. n. m. Shaving of the eyebrows and whiskers; and generally, shaving of the head, beard, eyebrows, and moustaches. chār ādmī, lit . four men. People in general; a jury of arbitrators. Jo chār ādmī kaheṅ so manzūr . chār-uṅgal, chavvā , n. m. A measure equal to four fingers' breadth; one girah , q. v. or 2½ inches. chār-āīnā , n. m. Armour. chār band , n. Joints; limbs. Kyoṅ yāro, is makāṅ meṅ ye kaisī chalī havā, Jo muflisī se hosh kisī kā nahīṅ baj…

adj. Four. چارآبرو chār-ābrū, s. m. A kind of dervis who shaves his eyebrows and whiskers. چارآنکهين chār-ānkheṅ (four eyes) s. f. See چارچشم chār-chashm. چارانگل chār-ungal, s. m. A palm (four inches). چارانگلي chār-unglī, s. f. Handʼs breadth. چارآينه chār-āʼīna, s. m. Iron armour. چاربالش chār-bālish, s. m. A throne, couch, or sofa. چاربانگ chār-bāng, adj. Sensible, quick, intelligent, alert. چاربيسي chār-bīsī, s. Fourscore. چارپا chār-pā, s. A quadruped: see چارپايه chār-pāya. چارپاره chār-pāra, adj. Divided into four, four-pieced. چارٿوک chār-ṭūk, adj. Broken (into four pieces). چارجامه chār-jāma, s. m. A kind of saddle (without a tree). چارچشم chār-chashm, s. f. Meeting, interview, the eyes of two people meeting. چارچند chār-chand, adj. Quadruple, fourfold. چارخانه chār-ḵẖāna, 1. adj. Chequered. 2. s. m. A kind of cloth. چارديواري chār-dīwār-ī, s. f. A court-yard, enclosure, area. …
s. (dakh.) A kind of dish or soup, mulligatawny ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

s.f. Full gallop; — adv. At full gallop
s.m. A kind of saddle made of cloth (lit. of four folds) and without a tree
s.m. The going (and returning) of the bride and bridegroom to their respective homes four times in the month after marriage

adj. Fourfold, four times (as much), quadruple
adj. & s.m. Chequered, checked; — chequered cloth; checked muslin; cloth ornamented with squares; tripe (of a ruminant)
s.m. lit. 'Four teeth'; a four-year old ( = ćār-sāl)
s.m. The four quarters of the globe

adj. For a few days, temporary, fleeting, transient
s.f. A courtyard, enclosure, area; a wall round a town, ramparts; — ćār-dīhāt, s.m. An estate formed of the land belonging to four villages
s.m. A mode of sitting (like that of a tailor at work); — ćār-zānū baiṭhnā, v.n. To squat, to sit cross-legged
s.m. A cross-road, two roads crossing each other; a square or court; — adv. On all sides, all round
adv. On all sides, all round, everywhere

s.f. A square, a quadrangle; — ćār-konī-kī ćārolī, s.f. The kernel of the Chironjia sapida or Buchanania latifolia
s.m. The four quarters of the globe, the whole world ( = ćār-dāṅg)
adj. Fourfold, four times; — ćār-mag̠z, s.m. A walnut; — an earthen ball with which children play
adj. The four, the whole four, all four
s.m. The four religious orders among Hindūs
s.m. The four chief things, viz. arth, dharm, kām, and moksh
s.m. The four yugas or epochs of the world, viz. the Satya or Krita, Treta, Dwāpar, and Kālī
s.f. The four states of man; the four states of a student of the Vedānta philosophy, viz. waking, sleeping, unconsciousness, and absorption into the one universal spirit of Brahma
adv. On all sides, all round, everywhere
adv. At full length, on the back, sprawling
adv. = ćāroṅ diśā, ćāroṅ or, qq.v.
Four verses of the Qǒrʼān (used as a charm)
adv. On all four sides, all around, in all directions
s.m. The four chief castes among the Hindūs
s.m. The four successors of Mohammad, viz. Abū-bakr, Omar, Osmān, and ʻAlī
s.m. A sect of Musalmāns who venerate equally the four successors of Mohammad (the term is applied by the Shīʻas to the Sunnīs)
s.m. A square silver coin (used as a charm to discover a thief)
ćār-ek, s.m. One part out of four, a fourth part.

ety: Prk. चरेअव्वअं; S. चरितव्य+कं, rt. चर्
def: s.m. Food (for cattle), fodder, forage, herbage; provisions; bait (for fish); a young plant
more: n. m. S. चर to eat. 1. Forage; fodder; bait for fish. 2. Provisions. Bahin kahe 'merā bīr pyārā,' Kāl kahe 'merā hai yeh chārā .' Kabīr. The sister says 'my brother own is he.' Says Death, ''tis he I've marked my prey to be.' chār'ū , n. m. A great eater. Chārū so bhārū . Prov. Who eats bears (burdens). narm chārā , Farinaceous or soft food; slops.
s. m. Remedy, &c. See P. and H. چاره chāra.

more: n. m. chiṛī , n. f. the hen sparrow. S. चटक 1. A cock sparrow. Chiṛā lāyā dāl kā dānā, chiṛyā lāī chāval kā dānā, dono ne mil-ke khichṛī pakāī . Kahānī. 2. The blue throats and calliope. Cyaneula suecica and calliope pectoralis , birds of the chat family found in the hills. They are brown, with bright colored throats, red or blue —winter visitors to the plains, retiring to the hills to breed.
subs: s.f. Grazing-ground; grazing-land, pasture; a meadow.

def: s.m. A lamp; a light
more: n. m. 1. A lamp. 1. Chirāg raushan, murād hāsil! Prov. Thy lamp place on the shrine, answered is prayer thine. 2. Ghar meṅ chirāg tak nahīṅ jaltā! Not even a light in his house! (He is extremely poor. Or, he is so stingy!). 3. Jis ghar meṅ chālīs din chirāg na jale bhūt kā dakhal ho jātā hai . Wom. Evil spirits enter the house in which no light has been burned for forty days. 2. ( Met .). The light of the house. A son. Ghar kā chirāg. chirāg battī, lit . a lamp and a wick. Lights. chirāg battī k. , v. a. To prepare the lamps for lighting. chirāg battī kā waqt , n. m. Candle light; evening. chirāg baṛhānā, bujhānā, ṭhanḍā, gul, yā khāmosh k., chirāg ko hāth denā , v. n. To put out the light. chirāg-pā , adj. 1. Rearing (a horse). 2. Turned upside down. Auṅdhe muṅh, chirāg-pā . Prov. The horse on his hind legs, the rider on his back. chirāg-pā h. , v. n. 1. def: v.t. (dialec.) = ćaṛhānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to ascend, or mount, or embark, &c.; to cause to rise, or increase, or swell, &c.; to raise, lift, take up; to advance, elevate, exalt, magnify; to offer up (oblations or sacrifice), to sacrifice, devote; to brace (a drum, &c.); to string (a bow); to bend; to pull, draw on or up; to cock; to put (on, upon, or into, -par, — as a utensil on a fire, or colour on a thing, or goods into a boat or ship, &c.); to spread, to apply (colour, or decorations, to, -par, cloth, &c.); to fix (a bayonet, &c.); to put in hand (a work); to raise to the mouth, to drink, imbibe; to lead (an army, against, -par)
more: (trans. of چڙنا) v. a. To vex, irritate, provoke, offend, mock, gibe, jeer.
v. a. (dakh.) See چڙهانا chaṛhānā. ¶ chuṛānā (dakh.) To make grimaces ( Harris ).

def: v.t. To tend grazing cattle, to graze, pasture; to feed; — to have (one) under command; to lead (one by the nose); to make a fool of.
more: v. a. P. charīdan , चर to eat. 1. To graze; pasture; feed. 1. Dadhī, mākhan ke chor ho, kahte bāt banāë, Sab chaturāī chhoṛ-ke, jāo charāo gāe! Krishn-līlā. You steal our butter and milk, and gammon with smooth phrase, Leave off your wily tricks, go to, your cattle graze! 2. Ab ḍhoroṅ nūṅ kaun charāve? Lalvā mūl na jāvegā, Ḍhor raheṅ saë din din bhūke, niyār kaṛe se āvegā? Rus. song. No one to graze the cattle now, this boy wont take them out, The tired cattle starve each day, no fodder to be got. 2. To mix up ( milānā ). Ṭeṭoṅ meṅ non charā do . 3. To command; lead by the nose. Woh aisoṅ ko charāë phirtā hai . 4. To make a fool of ( ahmaq banānā ).

v. a. S. चुर to steal; Pālī, choretī . 1. To steal; filch; cabbage; commit theft. 1. Guā'in se Krishn jī kaheṅ madhurī boli meṅ, 'E, kahā churāë jāt ho tum cholī meṅ? ' Thus Krishn to the milkmaids spoke in honeyed accents sweet, 'Some thing you've stolen and hid within your bosom's safe retreat?' 2. Bājū-band bhūli palaṅg par, pāve tau denā, mahāra, Sās morī dūtī, nanad morī bai ān, sautan līyo hai churāë . Song. Ṭhumrī . Give me, my lord, my armlet on the bed forgotten left, wife keen for theft. Your mother a spy, your sister my foe, my co- 3. Hāth lakaṛyā, kāṅdhe kamalyā, ban meṅ gauveṅ charāve . Song. Kanhaiyā. 2. To embezzle; misappropriate. 3. To absorb; suck in; take in; swallow. Pānī churānā . 4. To commit plagiary; to plagiarize. Jo churāte haiṅ mere mazmūn-i-nau ijād ko, Khānā-dāmād apnā karte haiṅ merī aulād ko .
v. n. 1. To crackle before breaking. 2. To throb; smart. Zakhm charrāyā jātā hai . 3. To cause to tear or to be torn. 4. To emit the sound made in tearing cloth.
( चारण or caus. of چرنا charnā ) v. a. To graze, to pasture, to feed.
v. a. To steal, filch, rob.
(causal of چيرنا) v. a. To cause to tear or be torn, cause to split.
v. n. 1. To burst. 2. To ache, to smart.

Mīr Hasan. charandam khurandam , Illit. n. m. Meat and drink. churandam khurandam k. , v. n. To eat; swallow; consume; spend. char'nī , n. f. A feeding trough.

more: adj. S. चारुवाक्य, P. charab . 1. Sharp; clever; sharp-witted; keen; canny; shrewd. 2. Bold; impudent; wanton; immodest. Hotā nahīṅ hai aisā bahū betiyoṅ kā taur, Charbāṅk dīdah hotā hai aksar chhināl kā . Wom. Jān Sāhab. The ways of modest wives and daughters are not so, These bold looks only frail and wanton women show.

more: n. m. S. चर्च to talk. Pr. champaī he studies. 1. Mention; talk; report; rumour; the talk of the town. 1. Dunyā ke jo maze haiṅ, hargiz yeh kam na hoṅge, Charchā yehī rahegā, afsos ham na hoṅge! Nazīr. The world's enjoyments all will still remain the same, All tongues will wag as now, alas! I nowhere in the game. 2. Koī din to yeh charchā rahā, 'hāthī milā, hāthī mílā!' Baiṭhā agar haude upar, yā pālkī meṅ jā chaṛhā, Āge ko naqqāre nishāṅ, pīchhe ko faujoṅ kā parā, Dekhā to phir ek ān meṅ hāthī, na ghoṛā, nā gadhā, Gar yūṅ huā to kyā huā, aur wūṅ huā to kyā huā ! On death. Nazīr. Brief space men's tongues may go—'an elephant he sports, look ho!' Albeit upon an elephant or pālkī borne, e'en so, Heralds and flags before, great retinue behind for show! But look once more, nor elephant, nor horse, nor ass here now, What boots it then at all if so you be, or you be so? 2. Gossip; scandal; obl…

s. m. f. 1. Talking over past events, mention, mentioning, report, discourse, discussion, consideration, careful perusal, argument, prevalence, engagedness, attention to business. 2. Adoration. 3. Perfuming by plastering the whole person with sandal-wood, &c.

subs: (ćarćā+āvali), s.f. Discourse, mention, recapitulation; story, history, account
(-kā), To make mention (of), to talk or discourse (of or about); to report, to give out; — ćarće-meṅ rahnā (-ke), To continue engaged in, to apply oneself constantly to, &c.

v.n. To crack, to crackle, &c. ( = ćarćarānā, q.v.). v.n. To chatter, prate, talk nonsense; to crackle (as burning wood), to sputter; to chew with a cracking noise; to creak (as shoes, &c.); to make a scratching noise (as a pen in writing); to be angry, peevish, or irritable, to fret, fume, storm; to be pungent or sharp (on the tongue); to smart, tingle, burn ( = ćarparānā).

v.n. To be risen, &c.; to be in practice, to be in good working condition (as the hand, eye, &c.); to be in hand (as a work), to be in course of preparation; to be possessed (by an evil spirit; e.g. uske sir jin ćaṛhā-huʼā hai).

def: s.m. A rider, a trooper, &c. ( = ćaṛhait, q.v.). s.m. A mounter, rider, horseman, trooper; an expert horseman.

s.f. Ascent; rise; ascension, &c. ( = ćaṛhāʼo); — conveying, conveyance; — embarkation; attack, assault, invasion, irruption; — price or fare for ascending, or riding, or embarking, &c., fare; passage-money.

ety: ćaṛh(nā)+ait = āʼit = Prk. आइत्तो = आवेंतो = S.आपयन् (the termin. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment आपि)
def: s.m. A mounter, rider, horseman, trooper; an expert horseman.
s.m. A rider, &c. ( = ćaṛhait); — a trooper who does not ride his own horse.
more: (from چڙهنا) s. m. A trooper who does not ride his own horse, a rider.

def: s.m. = ćaṛhwaiyā, q.v. s.m. One who ascends or mounts; a climber; a rider, horseman; one who places goods or grain, &c. in the scale for weighment.
s.m. Ascending; riding &c.

v.t. To cause to ascend, or mount, or embark, &c.; to cause to rise, or increase, or swell, &c.; to raise, lift, take up; to advance, elevate, exalt, magnify; to offer up (oblations or sacrifice), to sacrifice, devote; to brace (a drum, &c.); to string (a bow); to bend; to pull, draw on or up; to cock; to put (on, upon, or into, -par, — as a utensil on a fire, or colour on a thing, or goods into a boat or ship, &c.); to spread, to apply (colour, or decorations, to, -par, cloth, &c.); to fix (a bayonet, &c.); to put in hand (a work); to raise to the mouth, to drink, imbibe; to lead (an army, against, -par)
more: (trans. of چڙهنا chaṛhnā ) v. a. 1. To make ascend, embark, &c. 2. To offer up oblations or sacrifice. 3. To string (a bow), to brace (a drum). 4. To raise, lift, take up, advance, apply, put, spread, bend, die (with colour), pull, draw on, cock, fix (a bayonet), run.
wety: Compare Punjabi چاڑھنا (cāṛhnā) / ਚਾੜ੍ਹਨਾ (cāṛhnā).

def: s.m. Ascent, rise, acclivity; rise (in prices, &c.); increase, augmentation; elevation, ascension; exaltation, promotion, advancement; rank, dignity; offering (made to idols = ćaṛhāwā); (in Math.) raising to a power, involution; swelling, inundation; flood-tide; up-stream; attack, assault, onset; access, fit; excitement, passion; loftiness, pride, conceit; intoxication; — string (of a bow)
subs: s.m. Ascent and descent; rise and fall; flood-tide and ebb-tide; up-hill and down-hill; up-stream and down-stream; ups and downs; (in Math.) involution and evolution; (in Mus) rising and descending scale.

def: s.m. A kind of jacket (worn by harlots).
adj. & adv. Capable of being ascended, or climbed, &c.; — in advance (as payment, &c.).
more: (from چڙهنا) s. m. Ascent, rise, acclivity, attack, access, fit, rank, dignity, flood-tide.

def: s.m. = ćaṛhauwā, q.v. s.m. A shoe with a high heel (in contradistinction to a ghetlā, or a slipper without a heel).
s.m. = ćaṛhauwā, q.v. s.m. A shoe with a high heel (in contradistinction to a ghetlā, or a slipper without a heel).
s.m. A shoe with a high heel (in contradistinction to a ghetlā, or a slipper without a heel).
s.m. Anything presented in sacrifice, a religious offering or gift; a present (from a bridegroom to his bride)
subs: To present or offer up a religious offering.

def: s.m. A shoe with a high heel (in contradistinction to a ghetlā, or a slipper without a heel).
more: adj. Rising up (a shoe over the heel of the wearer).

def: s.f. = ćaṛhnī, q.v. s.f. Preparation for battle.
more: s. f. Preparations for battle. ¶ chuṛhī, s. (dakh.) See S̱. چڙيا chiṛiyā.

def: adv. With progressive or steady rise or increase; with raised voice, in a loud voice, aloud, in a high tone; confessedly, avowedly, designedly; in a superior manner or degree, excellently; — ćaṛh-ćaṛh-kar, adv. intens. of and = ćaṛhkar
subs: v.n. To speak with raised voice, or in a high tone, &c.

def: adv. With progressive or steady rise or increase; with raised voice, in a loud voice, aloud, in a high tone; confessedly, avowedly, designedly; in a superior manner or degree, excellently; — ćaṛh-ćaṛh-kar, adv. intens. of and = ćaṛhkar
subs: v.n. To speak with raised voice, or in a high tone, &c.

ety: ćaṛh˚ = Prk. चड्ढ(इ), or चड्ड(इ) = S. उद्+शद(ति; i.e. उच्छदति)
def: v.n. To go up, ascend, mount, climb; to soar; to ride (on, -par); to go (on board), embark; to go, be taken or conveyed, be transported (to); to go (on), fit; to go forward, advance, proceed (as a procession); to come on (as fever, &c.); to rise (as a price, a river, &c.), to increase, swell; to accumulate, fall in arrears (as pay. &c.); to be enlarged, to spread, to grow; to be raised, elevated, exalted, promoted; to be offered (as a sacrifice); to be drawn on or up, be tucked up; to be strung (as a bow); to be braced (as a drum, &c.); to be fixed (as a bayonet, or an arrow); to be set (as a bone); to be put (on, or in, as colour; or a weight, &c. in a scale, &c.), to be applied, to be spread or laid (over); to be entered (in, as an account or item), be put down; to march (against), lead an attack (against); to invade; to enter (the mind); to enter (the system, or head, &c.) and take effect (as poison, intoxicants, fever, &c.); to be possessed (by an evil spirit); to be taken, be caught, be hooked, be entrapped; to be superior (to), to excel; to gain (as a watch); to pass, elapse (as time, e.g. pahar-bhar din ćaṛhā); to be put in hand, be taken in hand (as a work)
more: v. n. 1. To ascend, mount, advance, attack, embark, board, rise, climb, soar, spread, swell, ride. 2. To be strung (a bow), to be braced (a drum). 3. To be offered up (an oblation or sacrifice). چڙهنا اترنا chaṛhnā utarnā, v. n. 1. To hire and dismiss. 2. Congressus.
subs: (-par), To come (against), to be led to the attack (of)
v.n. To ascend and descend; to be advanced and lowered; to be hired and dismissed; — congressus
v.n. To advance, gain ground, to get the better (of), to win, score (a victory); to find an opportunity

def: s.f. Preparation for battle.

adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Ascending, rising, increasing, waxing (as the moon), advancing, progressing, progressive, growing; increasing in speed, accelerated; superior (to, -se), better, excelling; — rise or increase (of price, &c., see ćaṛhtī); settlement of revenue at a progressively increasing rate
subs: s.m. A rise, or an advance, in price
s.m. A lease for a term of years at a progressively increasing rent
v.n. To be rising, &c.; to be superior (to, -se), be better (than), to excel.

ety: ćaṛh(nā)+tī = atī = Prk. अत्तिआ = S. आपि caus. augment+ति+का (or आप्तिका)
def: s.f. Rise, increase, advance; superiority; advantage, profit, gain.
s.m. One who ascends or mounts; a climber; a rider, horseman; one who places goods or grain, &c. in the scale for weighment.
more: (from چڙهنا) s. m. One who ascends, a mounter, rider, climber.

def: v.t. To cause to be raised, to have (a thing, &c.) raised sent up, pulled on, put on, &c. (see ćaṛhnā).
more: (caus. of چڙهنا) v. a. To cause others to raise or to make ascend or to pull on, &c.

def: s.f. Raising rents.
s.f. Green corn cut for fodder; — a small portion of land held rent-free by a cultivator ( = ćarotar).
more: n. f. Corn sown thick and cut unripe for fodder.
n. f. The leg-trick. Ek chaṛī meṅ auṅdhe mūṅh āyā .
n. f. S. चटका, Pālī. chaṭako . A bird. chiṛī laṛānā , See āg lagānā .
s. f. Unripe corn cut or the food of cattle. ¶ churī ( चूडा ) A kind of bracelet.

def: adj. Movable, locomotive; unsteady, shaking, tremulous.
s.f. The leg-trick (in wrestling), tripping.
more: s. f. 1. See چوڙي chūṛī. 2. (dakh.) See S̱. چڙيا chiṛiyā.
s. f. (fem. of چڙا) A hen-sparrow.

def: part. & s.m. Gone; gone to, attained, gone over or through, done; enacted; practised; customary; — acting, doing, practice, behaviour, &c. ( = ćaritr, q.v.); a fixed institute, proper or peculiar observance; disposition of mind
subs: (ćarita+artha), adj. Attaining one's object or end, successful in an undertaking, successful; effected; satisfied ( = kr̤itārth).

def: s.m. Acting, behaving; behaviour, conduct, carriage, bearing, procedure, manner, way; habit, practice, custom, usage; acts, deeds, proceedings; arts, tricks, wiles; exploits, adventures; story, history, or account of any one's deeds or exploits; nature, temper, disposition; quality; talent
more: n. m. S. चर to move; Pālī, chārittam . 1. Story; history; legend; drama. 2. Wiles; tricks; manœuvres; arts. chalittar-hāī , n. f. A cunning artful woman. Mar rī, nār chalittar-hāī, auṅṭhī bāt banāve nā, Bahuaṛ kār kareṅ sab ghar ghar, sāsū hāth lagāve nā . Rus. song. Death take thee, scheming minx! your idle feints wont draw, In every house the young wife works, and not the mother-in-law. tiryā charittar , Woman's artful schemes and wiles.
s. m. Conduct, behaviour, disposition, nature, procedure, manner, carriage, quality, custom, talent; history.
subs: (i.e. strīya ćaritra), s.m. Woman's ways; woman's wiles.

def: adj. & s.m. White-spotted (as from leprosy); — morbid whiteness of the skin, white leprosy; a slight wound, a cut, a scratch; — cauterization; — damage, loss; fraud, swindle.
more: n. m. 1. A cut; prick; scratch. 2. Cauterization. 3. Damage; loss. 4. Swindle; fraud.
1. adj. Whitespotted (as, from a leprosy). 2. s. m. A small wound, scratch.

def: s.m. = ćarkā, q.v. adj. & s.m. White-spotted (as from leprosy); — morbid whiteness of the skin, white leprosy; a slight wound, a cut, a scratch; — cauterization; — damage, loss; fraud, swindle.

ety: ćāra+kāṭā = kāṭ˚+ā = iʼā = iwā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. The man who cuts forage for cattle, elephants, &c.; elephant-keeper's mate.
more: s. m. The person who cuts forage for cattle, elephant-keeperʼs mate.

def: s.m. A wheel (as of a water-mill, or of a well, &c.); a potter's wheel; a lathe; the celestial globe or orb, the sphere of the heavens, the heavens, the sky; — circular motion; turn; — fortune, chance; — a cross-bow; a bow in a very tense state; — a falcon (particularly a white one); a hyena
more: n. m. S. चक्र 1. The celestial globe; heavenly sphere; the sky; the heavens. 2. A lathe. 3. A hawk. 4. A hyæna. charkh-pūjā , Cor. of chakr-pūjā . A ceremony observed by the lower orders of Hindus in Bengal, on the day when the sun enters Aries, for the expiation of their sins. The performer is suspended by an iron hook passed through the skin of the back, to one end of the lever, which is raised on the top of a high pole and whirled round by means of a rope fixed to the other end. This penance is performed for rich people by deputy. charkh chaṛhānā , v. a. To turn on a lathe. charkh chaṛhnā , v. n. To be turned (as in a lathe). charkh khānā , v. n. 1. ( chakrānā ) To turn round; circulate; revolve. 2. To be formed into globules (as quicksilver). charkh hiṅḍolā . A merry-go-round.

s. m. 1. A wheel (particularly, a potterʼs, or of a watermill, a well, or an oil-press). 2. The sky, the heavens, the celestial globe, the sphere. 3. Circular motion. 4. Turn, press. 5. Fortune, chance. 6. An hyena. جرخ اثير charḵẖ-i-as̤īr, The heaven of the stars. چرخ اطلس charḵẖ-i-at̤las, The crystalline sphere or heaven. چرخ پوجا charḵẖ-pūjā, A ceremony observed by the lower orders of Hindūs in Bengal, on the day when the sun enters Aries, for the expiation of their sins. They are suspended, by an iron hook passed through the skin of the back, to one end of a lever, which is raised on the top of a high pole, and whirled round by means of a rope fixed to the other end. چرخ چڙهنا charḵẖ chaṛhnā, v. n. 1. To dress or adorn oneʼs self. 2. To turn (as, in a lathe). چرخ دوار charḵẖ-i-dauwār, s. m. The sky, the heavens, the firmament. چرخ دولابي charḵẖ-i-dolābī, s. The sky, the celestial orb…
subs: s.m. The ethereal sphere; the heaven of the stars
s.m. The blue vault, the ethereal sphere; — ćarḵẖ-ě-at̤las, s.m. The crystalline sphere or heaven (where the Mohammadans imagine the throne of God is fixed)
s.m. A bowman; an expert archer

ćaraḵẖ-pūjā, s.f. A ceremony observed by the lower orders of Hindūs on the day when the sun enters Aries, for the expiation of their sins (they are suspended, by an iron hook thrust through the flesh of the back, to one end of a lever which is raised on the top of a high pole, and whirled round by means of a rope attached to the other end: this penance is often performed by proxy for those who are rich enough to pay for it)
v.n. To be turned (as on a lathe); to dress or adorn (oneself)
ćarraḵẖ-ćūṅ, s.f. The creaking of a wheel (cf. ćar-ćūṅ)
s.m. Revolving heaven; the sky, the firmament; changeable fortune
s.m. The celestial orb; the sky
adj. & s.m. Whirling, turning, revolving; — one who turns a wheel; a spinner
v.n. To be turned; to turn round, whirl, revolve, rotate; to circulate; to be formed into globules (as quicksilver, &c.); to be tested (by fire, as metals)
v.n. To wheel, turn round, revolve, to move or soar in a circle; to circulate (as the blood)

def: s.m. = ćarḵẖā, q.v. s.m. A spinning-wheel; a reel; the axis of a pulley; — a nag; jade, garran; — adj. Thin, weak, poor, sorry; worn out, rickety (as a cart, &c.)
subs: ćarḵẖā-ponī, ćarḵẖā-o-pūnī, s.f. Spinning and carding
v.n. To become worn out, rickety, weak, old, &c.
